authoritative_sources.title: Franchise Rule Compliance Guide
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC is the primary federal regulator for the Franchise Rule and this guide directly governs FDD timing, franchise seller conduct, contradictory statements, and written substantiation for financial performance representations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Federal disclosure timing, seller conduct, and disclosure-integrity control

authoritative_sources.title: Amended Franchise Rule FAQ's
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC staff FAQs provide authoritative public interpretation on Item 23 receipt handling, seller identification, Item 19 placement, exclusive-territory disclosures, and how state-driven FDD revisions affect other states.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Receipt handling, territory-disclosure handling, and FDD consistency review

authoritative_sources.title: 2008 Franchise Registration and Disclosure Guidelines
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NASAA's guidelines are the recognized filing-state workflow baseline for franchise registration forms, seller disclosure forms, state cover pages, receipt placement, and master or multi-unit registration treatment.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Filing-state application assembly, state cover page, and receipt-control handling

authoritative_sources.title: Multi-Unit Commentary
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NASAA's multi-unit commentary is a recognized public workflow reference for distinguishing area development, area representative, master-franchise, and subfranchise structures and the extra disclosure and registration boundaries they create.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Multi-unit structure classification and adjacent-authority routing

authoritative_sources.title: Statement of Policy Regarding the Use of Franchise Questionnaires and Acknowledgments
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NASAA's policy sets recognized control expectations for what questionnaires and acknowledgments may require, what must be attached in Item 22, and which waiver-like statements support staff must not collect from prospects.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Evidence capture, questionnaire control, and anti-waiver failure handling

authoritative_sources.title: About the Franchise Investment Law
authoritative_sources.publisher: California Department of Financial Protection and Innovation
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DFPI is an authoritative franchise filing-state regulator and this page states California's registration-before-offer-or-sale requirement and pre-sale disclosure and final-agreement delivery timing.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Filing-state readiness, disclosure-timing review, and California-specific escalation

authoritative_sources.title: Instructions on How to File a Complete Franchise Application
authoritative_sources.publisher: California Department of Financial Protection and Innovation
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DFPI's filing instructions define required document bundles, completeness standards, clean and marked-copy expectations, seller disclosure forms, audited financial statements, and advertisement filing controls relevant to franchise development support evidence handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Application completeness, filing evidence validation, and deficiency escalation

workflow_stages.name: Request intake and jurisdiction grounding
workflow_stages.description: Confirm the brand, filing state, deal structure, candidate identity, seller or broker identity, and requested milestone before responding.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Disclosure, filing, and evidence validation
workflow_stages.description: Validate the governing FDD version, Item 23 receipt date, state effective date, seller disclosure form, application completeness, and any filing-state instructions that materially change timing or document requirements.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Candidate, territory, and milestone administration
workflow_stages.description: Map candidate status, territory packaging, discovery-day scheduling, committee-prep artifacts, and owner-tracking into a bounded program-status or resolution output.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Communication and approved next-step packaging
workflow_stages.description: Prepare candidate or stakeholder communications that stay within approved wording, avoid unsupported Item 19 or territory claims, and identify the next owner when approval is outside the lane.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Exception, repeat-failure, and adjacent-authority escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate unsupported workarounds, stale-disclosure cases, multi-unit structure shifts, policy exceptions, and repeated filing or evidence failures into explicit handoff packets.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: FDD versions, final agreement versions, and Item 23 receipts
state registration or exemption status records, state effective-date pages, and examiner correspondence
franchise seller disclosure forms, broker or referral records, and discovery-day communications
territory request worksheets, market-availability records, and multi-unit or area-development structure summaries
committee packets, owner-tracking logs, due-date trackers, and escalation notes
questionnaires, acknowledgments, Item 22 exhibits, and communication templates
audited financial statement packages, addenda, and application completeness checklists
systems_of_record: state filing portals and correspondence channels such as FRANSES, EFD, or examiner email
candidate checklist and approval workflow system
calendar or event workflow for discovery day and committee milestones

allowed: Classify requests as in scope, out of scope, blocked, or approval-bound.
Request the minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, and source context needed to continue safely.
Provide evidence-backed status, due-date, and next-step summaries without overstating approval or legal authority.
Prepare escalation packets and handoff summaries when ownership shifts.
escalate: Required jurisdiction, filing-state, FDD, receipt, seller, territory, or approval records are missing or materially conflict.
The request requires legal interpretation, candidate approval, territory commitment, exemption analysis, contract negotiation, or Item 19 claim approval.
A multi-unit, area-development, area-representative, master-franchise, or subfranchise structure changes the disclosure or registration model.
Public baseline guidance and tenant, filing-state, or examiner direction materially conflict.
refuse: Promise earnings, returns, or territory protection not supported by approved disclosures and authority.
Advance signing or approval timing ahead of required disclosure or filing controls.
Require a prospect to sign waiver-like questionnaires, acknowledgments, or non-reliance statements outside approved policy.

domain_failure_modes: the lane advances a candidate using a stale FDD, missing Item 23 receipt, or expired filing status
support relays earnings claims, unit economics, or territory assurances that are outside approved Item 19 or legal-reviewed materials
multi-unit, area-development, area-representative, or subfranchise structures are handled like ordinary single-unit administration
seller or broker identity and state disclosure obligations are not tied to the candidate record
questionnaires or acknowledgments are used as waiver devices instead of controlled evidence artifacts
repeat filing or disclosure failures are treated as ordinary status work instead of escalation-worthy control breakdowns
the industry label is cosmetic and the pack collapses into generic sales support rather than franchise-development evidence handling

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Customer or stakeholder requests a policy exception
Service request lacks the detail needed for safe resolution
Communication becomes a handoff to another lane

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Filing-state rules vary by jurisdiction and by structure, including seller registration expectations, timing statements, addenda, anti-waiver enforcement, and whether a multi-unit structure requires separate registration or specialized treatment.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: This pack uses FTC, NASAA, and California DFPI sources as the public baseline. Runtime must retrieve the actual filing state, current effective filing status, legal-approved state guidance, and deal structure before applying state-specific outcomes.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific development committee thresholds, broker compensation controls, territory hold windows, and candidate-finance qualification rules can materially narrow the public baseline.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The lane remains validated because these are explicit tenant prerequisites, not gaps in the public baseline. The specialist escalates when tenant policy is unavailable or conflicts with public-source constraints.

notes: The research baseline covers workflow, evidence, terminology, and failure behavior instead of only generic sales-administration scripting.

No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for validated packaging because jurisdiction-specific legal interpretation and tenant approval differences are explicitly handled through runtime retrieval and escalation rules.

The lane stops at support administration and evidence-backed communication. Candidate approval, territory commitment, contract negotiation, legal interpretation, and financial performance representation approval remain outside the lane.
